summary: The chain builds cleanly to the target. Reading top to bottom, communication-protocol and data-packets are honest 'already covered' stubs with working links to ../../registry/.../figure/figure.html; unreliable-delivery then shows messages getting lost/corrupted/reordered with no warning; delivery-acknowledgement introduces the got-it reply, timeout-wait, and resend built directly on that failure picture; and the TCP target composes greet (syn/syn-ack/ack), talk (per-packet ack + resend), and goodbye (fin/ack) into one arc, self-glossing every piece of jargon inline. No unexplained leaps: each idea the target leans on (packets, protocol, acknowledgement) appears earlier and is linked, and TCP-specific terms (handshake, syn, ack, fin) are defined in plain language within the figure. A curious adult could follow it start to finish and understand the TCP connection lifecycle.
gaps: Minor (not blocking): computer-network is never illustrated — it is a direct prerequisite of unreliable-delivery but is left to a later run. It is honestly stubbed in two places (the unreliable-delivery 'Builds on' list and the Frontier section), and the unreliable-delivery figure itself draws a 'network' band with 'many hops the sender does not control', which supplies enough of a working mental model that the reader is not actually blocked.
